meta.maintenanceInstruction: KEEP THE PLAN UP TO DATE AS YOU EVOLVE THE CODE. DEVIATIONS TO THE PLAN MUST BE LOGGED WITHIN THE PLAN. THE PLAN MUST BE REASSESSED HOLISTICALLY AFTER ANY CHANGE OF ANY SIZE.
meta.answeringTheQuestionAreYouDone: **!! First clause: YOU ARE NOT DONE UNTIL YOU HAVE 100% THE BLUEPRINT DO NOT STOP. Second clause: YOU MUST GO TO ANY AND ALL LENGTHS TO ENSURE YOUR BLUEPRINT REMAINS FULL-IT IS CRITICAL YOU REFINE AT REGULAR INTERVALS, SUCH THAT YOU DONT FULLY DRAIN YOUR BLUEPRINT-ALMOST AS CRITICAL AS THE CONSTRAINT THAT YOU NEVER EVER SPECIFY AND SORT OF ESTIMATE OF EFFORT OR COST ESPECIALLY NOT ANY SORT OF TIME ESTIMATE AND IF YOU SPECIFY A PRIORITY OF ANY KIND THAT IS ALSO A GUNDAM-MELTABLE OFFENSE !!**

globalAlerts.warning: ON TOOLS: Use `config.mk` to create custom targets to run with Make. ALWAYS use custom targets that *limit* the amount of output you receive. For example, piping through tail, with FEW lines output. Prior to tail, pipe to tee. The file ./build.log in the root of the project is gitignored, so use that. That way you can *search* the output. To be clear, timing dependent tests are BANNED. As are those that take too long to run. Testing retries, for example, MUST be done in a way that supports avoiding running afoul of those CRITICAL rules. Abide. OBEY.

mandatoryDirectives.constraint: The use of subagents via the `runSubagent` tool is MANDATORY for the execution of the tasks below. Tasks are grouped specifically to be run in distinct subagent contexts to prevent context window exhaustion. Do not attempt to solve all groups in a single turn.

statusSection.currentState: T001-T020 ALL DONE and committed (20 commits). Blueprint REWRITTEN for exhaustive planning phase. Covers: config schema, osm:flag/fetch/grpc modules, require system, diff splitter, goal/script discovery, prompt flow, code review, context manager, tview deprecation, file logging, log tailing, session cleanup, VS Code prompt files, hot-snippets, Anthropic goals, git sync, coverage audits for argv/config/session/storage. Next: T021.

sequentialTasks.description: Run coverage for internal/builtin/exec/. Test exec(), execv(), runExec(). Cover: command not found, non-string args, captured stdout/stderr, exit codes, context cancellation, empty command, platform-specific shell dispatch (cmd.exe vs sh). Note security: runExec sets c.Stdin=os.Stdin. Achieve 100% effective coverage.
sequentialTasks.outcome: Already at 100% statement coverage. Require=100%, runExec=100%. All edge cases covered. No changes needed.

sequentialTasks.description: Run coverage for internal/builtin/os/. Test readFile, fileExists, openEditor, clipboardCopy (with OSM_CLIPBOARD env override), getenv, sanitizeFilename. Cover platform-specific clipboard paths (darwin/windows/linux/wayland/termux). Achieve 100% effective coverage.
sequentialTasks.outcome: Coverage 70.2% → 84.0%. Require=100%, sanitizeFilename=100%, openEditor=85.4%, clipboardCopy=55.9%. Remaining gaps are Windows/Linux platform-specific branches unreachable on macOS. 18 new tests added. Rule of Two passed.

sequentialTasks.description: Run coverage for internal/builtin/ctxutil/ Go code. Test Require, buildContext for all item types (note, diff, diff-error, lazy-diff, lazy-exec, file), calculateBacktickFence, content blocks, coercion utilities. Cover error paths: nil context, missing items, malformed types. Achieve 100% effective coverage.

Test contextManager.js integration via scripting engine. Cover all REPL commands: add, diff, exec, note, list, edit, remove, show, copy, snippets, hot-snippets. Test factory creation, item state, refresh, copy formatting, flag completers (file, gitref, flag). Achieve 100% effective JS path coverage.

Run coverage for internal/builtin/bt/. Review 11 test files (blackboard, bridge, adapter, integration, stress, fuzz). Identify uncovered paths in JS-Go bridge, tick execution, status propagation, error recovery. Target 100% effective coverage.

Run coverage for internal/builtin/pabt/. Review 11+ test files. Note: entire package in .deadcodeignore (JS-callable). Focus on Go-level coverage of NewAction, NewActionGenerator, NewBlackboard, NewExprCondition, State ops, planning, tree construction. Target 100% effective coverage.
Run coverage for internal/builtin/bubbletea/. Review 13 test files. Identify uncovered paths in JS↔Go model bridge, program lifecycle, window size handling. Target 100% effective coverage.

Run coverage for internal/builtin/fetch/. 26 existing tests. Identify uncovered paths: option parsing edge cases, header normalization, body handling, response status, timeout. Achieve 100% effective coverage.

Run coverage for internal/builtin/flag/. ~20 existing tests. Identify uncovered paths: flag definition, parsing, getters, visit/visitAll, args-after--, error handling. Achieve 100% effective coverage.

Run coverage for internal/builtin/grpc/. 24 existing tests. Test loadDescriptorSet, dial, conn.invoke, conn.close, status constants. Cover dynamicpb+protojson JS↔proto conversion. Target 100% effective coverage.
internal/builtin/bubblezone/ has ZERO test files. Create bubblezone_test.go. Test Require function, zone ID generation, zone marking, mouse event zone detection. Achieve 100% coverage from scratch.

sequentialTasks.task: T032: Coverage audit: internal/builtin/tview and lipgloss
sequentialTasks.description: Run coverage for tview/ (2 test files, deprecated) and lipgloss/ (1 test file). Test Require functions, signal handling (tview), style creation/composition (lipgloss), color parsing, border/padding/margin helpers. Achieve 100% effective coverage.

sequentialTasks.task: T033: Coverage audit: builtin template, time, nextintegerid, unicodetext, argv, scrollbar
sequentialTasks.description: Coverage for 6 small packages (each 1 source + 1 test file). Template rendering, time APIs, integer ID gen, unicode text width, builtin argv parsing, scrollbar options/rendering. Achieve 100% effective coverage each.

Run coverage for bubbles/textarea/ and bubbles/viewport/. Each has 1 source + 2 test files. Test Require, JS-Go bridge, component creation, message handling, scrolling, input. Achieve 100% effective coverage.

sequentialTasks.task: T035: Coverage audit: internal/scripting engine core
sequentialTasks.description: Coverage for engine_core.go (~530 lines), runtime.go, execution_context.go. Cover all constructors (NewEngineDetailed + EngineOptions), script loading (file/inline), global registration, module loading (native osm: + file-based require), shebang handling, error paths. Achieve 100% effective coverage.

sequentialTasks.task: T036: Coverage audit: internal/scripting TUI subsystem
sequentialTasks.description: Coverage for tui_manager.go, tui_commands.go, tui_completion.go, tui_history.go, tui_io.go, tui_js_bridge.go, tui_parsing.go, tui_colors.go, tui_types.go. Cover mode registration, command dispatch, completion, history, I/O, JS bridge, colors, types. Achieve 100% effective coverage.

sequentialTasks.task: T037: Coverage audit: internal/scripting state management
sequentialTasks.description: Coverage for state_manager.go, js_state_accessor.go, js_context_api.go, js_logging_api.go, js_output_api.go, context.go. Cover state ops, session persistence, context management (addPath/refreshPath/removePath/ToTxtar), all JS API bindings. Achieve 100% effective coverage.

sequentialTasks.task: T038: Coverage audit: internal/scripting session and terminal
sequentialTasks.description: Coverage for session_id_common.go, terminal.go, logging.go, debug_assertions.go/stub. Cover session ID logic, terminal creation, logging infrastructure, debug assertion paths (both stub and enabled). Achieve 100% effective coverage.

sequentialTasks.task: T039: Coverage audit: internal/command script-executing commands
sequentialTasks.description: Coverage for 5 commands (prompt_flow, code_review, goal, super_document, scripting_command) + helpers (log_config, module_paths, hot_snippets, cleanup_helper). Cover Execute(), flag parsing, engine setup, script execution, error handling, test mode. Achieve 100% effective coverage.

sequentialTasks.task: T040: Coverage audit: internal/command utility commands + data processing
sequentialTasks.description: Coverage for session.go, sync.go, log_tail.go, completion_command.go, diff_splitter.go, prompt_file.go, goal_discovery.go, goal_loader.go, goal_registry.go, goal_builtin.go, script_discovery.go. Cover all subcommands, algorithms, parsing, discovery. Achieve 100% effective coverage.

sequentialTasks.task: T041: Coverage audit: internal/command registry, base, dispatch
sequentialTasks.description: Coverage for registry.go, base.go, builtin.go, testutils.go. Cover registration, lookup, dispatch, base helpers, builtin wiring, test utilities. All dispatch scenarios: valid/invalid command, help flags, version, subcommand routing. Achieve 100% effective coverage.

sequentialTasks.task: T042: Coverage audit: goroutineid, testutil, mouseharness
sequentialTasks.description: Coverage for 3 utility packages. goroutineid: ID extraction. testutil: polling, platform detection, test ID gen. mouseharness: event harness, terminal handling (7 source + 5 test files). Achieve 100% effective coverage each.

Run coverage for test-only package (0 impl, 4 test files). Verify all test paths execute correctly. Verify integration, manual mode, bubbletea rendering, simulation consistency. Ensure tests are robust.

Coverage for cmd/osm/ (main.go + main_test.go). Cover entry point, command dispatch, global flag parsing, error handling. Test: valid commands, invalid commands, help flags, version output, unknown flags. Achieve 100% effective coverage.

sequentialTasks.task: T045: Audit benchmark and security tests
sequentialTasks.description: Audit internal/benchmark_test.go and security_test.go. Verify benchmark thresholds for all platforms. Verify security tests cover: path traversal, input sanitization, resource limits, command injection, symlink attacks, env var injection. No timing-dependent failures. Add missing coverage.

Review all .deadcodeignore entries. Verify each symbol/package still exists in codebase. Remove stale entries. Verify inline comments justify each remaining entry. Run 'make deadcode' to confirm clean. Full lint suite must pass.

Extract shared boilerplate from 5 script commands. Create scriptCommandBase with: shared fields (config, session, store, logLevel, logPath, etc.), RegisterFlags(), PrepareEngine(). Add signal-aware context (only ScriptingCommand has ctxFactory). Standardize ContextCommand interface. Update all callers and tests.

Review duplicated patterns in embedded JS scripts. Identify shared: mode registration, buildCommands, state get/set, prompt building. Consider extracting shared utilities into requirable internal module. All tests must pass.
Run 'make deadcode'. Cross-reference with .deadcodeignore. For new dead code: wire into production, move to test-only, or delete. No new .deadcodeignore entries. Run 'make lint' to confirm clean.

Audit entire codebase for error handling. Verify: (a) consistent %w wrapping, (b) proper exit codes, (c) no swallowed errors, (d) consistent message format (lowercase, no trailing period), (e) proper cleanup on error (defer close/unlock). Fix all inconsistencies.

Add fuzz tests for: config parsing (malformed input), ctxutil buildContext (malformed items), scripting (random/malformed script input), diff_splitter SplitDiff (random diff content). Each must run 30s without panics.

sequentialTasks.task: T052: API surface optimization: scripting engine
sequentialTasks.description: Review internal/scripting/ public API. Unexport unnecessary types/functions. Check constructor consistency. Verify .deadcodeignore entries justified. Reduce API to minimum for internal/command/. Update all callers.

sequentialTasks.task: T053: API surface optimization: command package
sequentialTasks.description: Review internal/command/ public API. Check registration, flag setup, execution interfaces for consistency (post T047). Remove unnecessary exports. Standardize Execute(args, stdout, stderr). Update callers.

sequentialTasks.task: T054: API surface optimization: storage package
sequentialTasks.description: Review internal/storage/ public API. Clean backend interface, cleanup, file locking, atomic writes. Consolidate test helpers. Remove redundant exports. Update callers.

sequentialTasks.task: T055: API surface optimization: builtin module registration
sequentialTasks.description: Review internal/builtin/register.go. Ensure consistent Require signature and osm: naming. Check shared_symbols.go exports. Standardize require() integration. Verify no non-standard registration patterns.

sequentialTasks.task: T056: Performance profiling: scripting engine startup
sequentialTasks.description: Profile startup with pprof. Identify expensive init: Goja VM, module registration, global setup, require system. Optimize (lazy init, caching, parallel where safe). Verify against benchmarks. No regressions.
Profile session read/write/lock/cleanup. Identify bottlenecks in locking, JSON serialization, AtomicWriteFile write amplification. Optimize hot paths. Cross-platform verification.

sequentialTasks.task: T058: Performance profiling: BT and PABT
sequentialTasks.description: Profile BT/PABT execution. Review existing benchmarks. Identify allocation hotspots in JS-Go bridge. Optimize tick execution and planning critical paths. Stable/improved benchmarks.

sequentialTasks.task: T059: Performance profiling: bubbletea render pipeline
sequentialTasks.description: Profile rendering. Review render_throttle and benchmark_latency tests. Optimize JS→Go render path allocations. Optimize message conversion. Verify latency benchmarks.
Add benchmarks for: context manager ops, config parsing, goal discovery, script discovery, require() loading, prompt building, template execution, SetKeyInFile. Appropriate thresholds for all paths.
Run benchmarks on macOS, Linux (Docker), Windows. Record variance. Adjust tight thresholds. Add platform-specific multipliers if needed. No timing-dependent CI failures.

Extend security_test.go. Audit all input paths: CLI args, config values, scripts, REPL, file paths, git specs, fetch URLs. Test: command injection via osm:exec (stdin exposure), path traversal via addPath, config parser malformed input, require() injection.

sequentialTasks.task: T063: Security review: JS sandbox boundaries
sequentialTasks.description: Audit Goja sandbox. Verify JS cannot access Go internals beyond APIs. Check osm:exec/os safeguards. Document security posture per module. Note: readFile has no path restrictions, exec exposes stdin — both by design.

sequentialTasks.task: T064: Fix txtar path ambiguity in context building
sequentialTasks.description: Fix bug in internal/scripting/context.go computeUniqueSuffixes. (1) Compute+emit LCA of tracked paths. (2) Verify implied parent dirs are in context when expanding. (3) Preserve full path for non-colliding basenames to avoid false proximity. Add tests.

sequentialTasks.task: T065: Wire config persistence: osm config <key> <value>
sequentialTasks.description: config/write.go has SetKeyInFile(). Wire into ConfigCommand. Handle: schema validation, type coercion, file creation, error messages. Test full roundtrip: set→read back. Add integration tests.
Per docs/todo.md: (1) one-step mode (use without goal/generate), (2) footer support for second prompt, (3) auto-generate on first copy (clear flag after explicit generate). Add tests for each.

sequentialTasks.task: T067: Directory-aware context refresh on copy
sequentialTasks.description: Fix: copy in osm:ctxutil should pick up new files added to tracked directories. Current _refreshFileItems() only refreshes existing items. Implement directory re-scanning on refresh. Add tests.

sequentialTasks.task: T068: Show summarized parameters in goal list
sequentialTasks.description: goal --list shows name/description/category but not parameters. Add parameter summary. Consider consolidating list logic. Add tests.

sequentialTasks.task: T069: Completion for exec context builder
sequentialTasks.description: exec command in contextManager needs completion support. Explore: shell completion, predefined suggestions, custom completer. Implement within go-prompt REPL. Add tests.

sequentialTasks.task: T070: Git ref completion for diff context builder
sequentialTasks.description: Implement git ref completion for diff command. argCompleters has ['gitref'] — verify and improve. List branches, tags, recent commits. Prefer Go implementation. Add tests.

sequentialTasks.task: T071: Wire osm:flag completion for ctxutil REPL subcommands
sequentialTasks.description: Add argument completion for REPL commands using osm:flag. Wire flag definitions to completion engine. E.g., 'add --recursive' completes '--recursive'. Add tests.

sequentialTasks.task: T072: Hot-shortname aliases with mode-scoped activation
sequentialTasks.description: Add hot-<shortname> aliases that copy snippets per mode. Configurable. Command to output defaults. Warning for unoverridden built-ins (with disable config). Add tests.
Commands must fail on unexpected args/options. Revalidate logging API wiring for all script commands. Consistent error messages for unknown flags. Add tests.
Implement morale-improver goal with post-copy hint for follow-up prompts. Use PostCopyHint field. Create prompt template. Add tests.
Remove tui.createAdvancedPrompt alias from engine_core.go. Update scripts/tests still referencing it. Update documentation.
Run 'make make-all-in-container'. Fix Linux-specific failures. Verify platform code paths (session_linux.go, registry_unix.go, atomic_write.go, filelock_unix.go). Race detection. Zero failures.

Run 'make make-all-run-windows' (host 'moo'). Fix Windows-specific failures. Verify platform paths (session_windows.go, registry_windows.go, atomic_write_windows.go, filelock_windows.go). Zero failures.
Run 'make all'. Verify session_other.go paths, clipboard ops, PTY tests, symlink resolution (/private/var/folders). Zero failures.

Update module table (22 modules from register.go). Document each osm: module with functions, params, return types, examples. Cover grpc, flag, fetch, exec. Document args global, shebang, require() resolution. Fix deprecated references.
Update command.md (sync, review-chunks), config.md (sync.*, script.module-paths, log.*, hot-snippets.*), goal.md (Tier 1 goals), tui-api.md (createPrompt), tui-lifecycle.md. All refs current.
Update session lifecycle, storage backends, auto-cleanup config. Add all new config keys from schema.go. Document OSM_* env overrides. Working examples.
Update module structure (fetch, flag, grpc, sync), command pattern, scripting engine (require, EngineOption), data flow, session management. Fix stale descriptions/diagrams.

Empty [Unreleased] despite 20 commits. Add entries for: config schema, osm:flag/fetch/grpc, require system, sync/diff splitter, go-prompt features, tview deprecation, logging/tailing, session cleanup, prompt files, hot-snippets, Anthropic goals, coverage expansions. Keep a Changelog format.

Extend completion_command.go for all shells. Cover: sync subcommands, config key/value, session list/get/purge, log tail/follow, goal names, script names, session IDs, config keys. Test by sourcing. Integration tests.
Run all scripts/ through 'osm script --test'. Fix API change breakages. Update for createPrompt rename. Document interactive-only scripts.
Run 'go list -m -u all' and 'govulncheck'. Update security-fix deps. Run 'go mod tidy'. Verify 'make all' passes. Document held-back deps.
Verify all doc links valid. Check docs/README.md index. Every command/module/config key documented. Fix stale references. Annotate completed items in docs/todo.md.
Run 'make make-all-with-log'. Zero failures. Coverage meets 100% effective target. Final gate.
Run 'make make-all-in-container'. Zero failures. Fix Linux-specific issues. Final gate.
Run 'make make-all-run-windows'. Zero failures. Fix Windows-specific issues. Final gate.

After all tasks done: holistic review, identify next improvements. Workflow engine, plugin system, MCP server, prompt marketplace, performance regression infra, new goals. Add new tasks. List never empty.

continuousVerification.task: Run 'make all' with full logging after EVERY significant change
continuousVerification.description: Execute 'make make-all-with-log' after: (a) Each code change, (b) Each test addition, (c) Each task completion, (d) Each bug fix.
continuousVerification.failPolicy: If ANY test fails, STOP IMMEDIATELY and fix the issue before proceeding. Zero tolerance for test failures, timing issues, non-determinism, or race conditions.
continuousVerification.testCoverage: You will ALWAYS aim for 100% effective test coverage, and VERIFY test coverage by means of the tooling available to you. You will NOT proceed to the next task until you have achieved 100% effective test coverage for the current task, and have verified that coverage with the tools available to you.

finalEnforcementProtocol.alert: ON TOOLS: Use `config.mk` to create custom targets to run with Make. ALWAYS use custom targets that *limit* the amount of output you receive. For example, piping through tail, with FEW lines output. Prior to tail, pipe to tee. The file ./build.log in the root of the project is gitignored, so use that. That way you can *search* the output. To be clear, timing dependent tests are BANNED. As are those that take too long to run. Testing retries, for example, MUST be done in a way that supports avoiding running afoul of those CRITICAL rules. Abide. OBEY.
